[[Category:new pages]] {{Citation needed|date=August 2015}}
'''Shōrokui''' (正六位) is one of the {{ill|Ranks of Japan|ja|位階}} and {{ill|Shinkai (divine rank)|lt=divine ranks|ja|神階}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位|1=Junior Fifth Rank}} and above {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank}}. In terms of decorations, it corresponds to the Fifth Class of {{ill|Orders of Merit|ja|勲等}} and the Fifth Class of the {{ill|Order of the Golden Kite|ja|金鵄勲章}}.
Under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, the Sixth Rank was granted to {{ill|Kaike|ja|介}}, who served as the deputy governor of lower provinces. It was classified as a rank for {{ill|Jige|ja|地下人}} (non-court nobles) and was distinctly separate from the Fifth Rank or higher, which denoted nobility ({{ill|Tsūki|ja|通貴}}). Those holding the Sixth Rank were not permitted {{ill|Shōden|ja|昇殿}} (entry to the palace). However, {{ill|Kurōdo|ja|蔵人}} (Chamberlains) were exceptions; their roles allowed them palace access, and those of the Sixth Rank were called {{ill|Rokui Kurōdo|ja|六位蔵人}}, collectively referred to as {{ill|Tenjōbito|ja|殿上人}} alongside those of the Fifth Rank or higher.
In terms of {{ill|Shinkai (divine rank)|lt=divine ranks|ja|神階}}, Shōrokui is the lowest rank. The {{ill|Samurai|ja|侍}} originated from the {{ill|Samuraihon|ja|侍品}} class, which consisted of individuals limited to the Sixth Rank who served as bureaucrats and aides to nobles. The Sixth Rank class was also referred to as {{ill|Aosamurai|ja|青侍}} (Blue Samurai) due to their {{ill|Po (clothing)|lt=Po robe|ja|袍|ja|袍}} being light blue in color.
In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}} and later, individuals holding ranks such as {{ill|Major|ja|少佐}} were appointed to this position.
Today, Shōrokui is posthumously conferred to individuals such as {{ill|Keishisei|ja|警視正}} (Senior Police Superintendent) in the {{ill|Japanese police|ja|日本の警察官}}, {{ill|Shōbōkan|ja|消防監}} (Fire Superintendent) in the {{ill|Firefighting personnel|ja|消防吏員}}, chairs of {{ill|Municipal councils in Japan|ja|日本の地方議会}} (city, town, and village councils), founders of special institutions and schools, and artists who have made significant contributions in their respective fields.
== External links == * [https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/document?lawid=215IO0000000325 位階令] - {{ill|e-Gov Law Search|ja|e-Gov法令検索}} * [{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 官位相当表] - National Diet Library Digital Archive

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|神階}}" to "|神階|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}}"
revision.text:
[[Category:new pages]] {{Citation needed|date=August 2015}}
'''Shōrokui''' (正六位) is one of the {{ill|Ranks of Japan|ja|位階}} and {{ill|Shinkai (divine rank)|lt=divine ranks|ja|神階|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位|1=Junior Fifth Rank}} and above {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank}}. In terms of decorations, it corresponds to the Fifth Class of {{ill|Orders of Merit|ja|勲等}} and the Fifth Class of the {{ill|Order of the Golden Kite|ja|金鵄勲章}}.
Under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, the Sixth Rank was granted to {{ill|Kaike|ja|介}}, who served as the deputy governor of lower provinces. It was classified as a rank for {{ill|Jige|ja|地下人}} (non-court nobles) and was distinctly separate from the Fifth Rank or higher, which denoted nobility ({{ill|Tsūki|ja|通貴}}). Those holding the Sixth Rank were not permitted {{ill|Shōden|ja|昇殿}} (entry to the palace). However, {{ill|Kurōdo|ja|蔵人}} (Chamberlains) were exceptions; their roles allowed them palace access, and those of the Sixth Rank were called {{ill|Rokui Kurōdo|ja|六位蔵人}}, collectively referred to as {{ill|Tenjōbito|ja|殿上人}} alongside those of the Fifth Rank or higher.
In terms of {{ill|Shinkai (divine rank)|lt=divine ranks|ja|神階|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}}, Shōrokui is the lowest rank. The {{ill|Samurai|ja|侍}} originated from the {{ill|Samuraihon|ja|侍品}} class, which consisted of individuals limited to the Sixth Rank who served as bureaucrats and aides to nobles. The Sixth Rank class was also referred to as {{ill|Aosamurai|ja|青侍}} (Blue Samurai) due to their {{ill|Po (clothing)|lt=Po robe|ja|袍|ja|袍}} being light blue in color.
In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}} and later, individuals holding ranks such as {{ill|Major|ja|少佐}} were appointed to this position.
Today, Shōrokui is posthumously conferred to individuals such as {{ill|Keishisei|ja|警視正}} (Senior Police Superintendent) in the {{ill|Japanese police|ja|日本の警察官}}, {{ill|Shōbōkan|ja|消防監}} (Fire Superintendent) in the {{ill|Firefighting personnel|ja|消防吏員}}, chairs of {{ill|Municipal councils in Japan|ja|日本の地方議会}} (city, town, and village councils), founders of special institutions and schools, and artists who have made significant contributions in their respective fields.
== External links == * [https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/document?lawid=215IO0000000325 位階令] - {{ill|e-Gov Law Search|ja|e-Gov法令検索}} * [{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 官位相当表] - National Diet Library Digital Archive

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Qq deprecated start]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
[[Category:new pages]] {{Citation needed|date=August 2015}}
'''Shōrokui''' (正六位) is one of the {{ill|Ranks of Japan|ja|位階}} and {{ill|Shinkai (divine rank)|lt=divine ranks|ja|神階|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位|1=Junior Fifth Rank}} and above {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank}}. In terms of decorations, it corresponds to the Fifth Class of {{ill|Orders of Merit|ja|勲等}} and the Fifth Class of the {{ill|Order of the Golden Kite|ja|金鵄勲章}}.
Under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, the Sixth Rank was granted to {{ill|Kaike|ja|介}}, who served as the deputy governor of lower provinces. It was classified as a rank for {{ill|Jige|ja|地下人}} (non-court nobles) and was distinctly separate from the Fifth Rank or higher, which denoted nobility ({{ill|Tsūki|ja|通貴}}). Those holding the Sixth Rank were not permitted {{ill|Shōden|ja|昇殿}} (entry to the palace). However, {{ill|Kurōdo|ja|蔵人}} (Chamberlains) were exceptions; their roles allowed them palace access, and those of the Sixth Rank were called {{ill|Rokui Kurōdo|ja|六位蔵人}}, collectively referred to as {{ill|Tenjōbito|ja|殿上人}} alongside those of the Fifth Rank or higher.
In terms of {{ill|Shinkai (divine rank)|lt=divine ranks|ja|神階|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}}, Shōrokui is the lowest rank. The {{ill|Samurai|ja|侍}} originated from the {{ill|Samuraihon|ja|侍品}} class, which consisted of individuals limited to the Sixth Rank who served as bureaucrats and aides to nobles. The Sixth Rank class was also referred to as {{ill|Aosamurai|ja|青侍}} (Blue Samurai) due to their {{ill|Po (clothing)|lt=Po robe|ja|袍|ja|袍}} being light blue in color.
In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}} and later, individuals holding ranks such as {{ill|Major|ja|少佐}} were appointed to this position.
Today, Shōrokui is posthumously conferred to individuals such as {{ill|Keishisei|ja|警視正}} (Senior Police Superintendent) in the {{ill|Japanese police|ja|日本の警察官}}, {{ill|Shōbōkan|ja|消防監}} (Fire Superintendent) in the {{ill|Firefighting personnel|ja|消防吏員}}, chairs of {{ill|Municipal councils in Japan|ja|日本の地方議会}} (city, town, and village councils), founders of special institutions and schools, and artists who have made significant contributions in their respective fields.
== External links == * [https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/document?lawid=215IO0000000325 位階令] - {{ill|e-Gov Law Search|ja|e-Gov法令検索}} * [{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 官位相当表] - National Diet Library Digital Archive

[[Category:new pages]] {{Citation needed|date=August 2015}}
'''Shōrokui''' (正六位) is one of the {{ill|Ranks of Japan|ja|位階}} and {{ill|Shinkai (divine rank)|lt=divine ranks|ja|神階|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位|1=Junior Fifth Rank}} and above {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank}}. In terms of decorations, it corresponds to the Fifth Class of {{ill|Orders of Merit|ja|勲等}} and the Fifth Class of the {{ill|Order of the Golden Kite|ja|金鵄勲章}}.
Under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, the Sixth Rank was granted to {{ill|Kaike|ja|介}}, who served as the deputy governor of lower provinces. It was classified as a rank for {{ill|Jige|ja|地下人}} (non-court nobles) and was distinctly separate from the Fifth Rank or higher, which denoted nobility ({{ill|Tsūki|ja|通貴}}). Those holding the Sixth Rank were not permitted {{ill|Shōden|ja|昇殿}} (entry to the palace). However, {{ill|Kurōdo|ja|蔵人}} (Chamberlains) were exceptions; their roles allowed them palace access, and those of the Sixth Rank were called {{ill|Rokui Kurōdo|ja|六位蔵人}}, collectively referred to as {{ill|Tenjōbito|ja|殿上人}} alongside those of the Fifth Rank or higher.
In terms of {{ill|Shinkai (divine rank)|lt=divine ranks|ja|神階|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}}, Shōrokui is the lowest rank. The {{ill|Samurai|ja|侍}} originated from the {{ill|Samuraihon|ja|侍品}} class, which consisted of individuals limited to the Sixth Rank who served as bureaucrats and aides to nobles. The Sixth Rank class was also referred to as {{ill|Aosamurai|ja|青侍}} (Blue Samurai) due to their {{ill|Po (clothing)|lt=Po robe|ja|袍|ja|袍}} being light blue in color.
In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}} and later, individuals holding ranks such as {{ill|Major|ja|少佐}} were appointed to this position.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "Minor Initial Rank" to "Lesser Initial Rank"
revision.text:
[[Category:new pages]] {{Citation needed|date=August 2015}}
'''Shōrokui''' (正六位) is one of the {{ill|Ranks of Japan|ja|位階}} and {{ill|Shinkai (divine rank)|lt=divine ranks|ja|神階|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位|1=Junior Fifth Rank}} and above {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank}}. In terms of decorations, it corresponds to the Fifth Class of {{ill|Orders of Merit|ja|勲等}} and the Fifth Class of the {{ill|Order of the Golden Kite|ja|金鵄勲章}}.
Under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, the Sixth Rank was granted to {{ill|Kaike|ja|介}}, who served as the deputy governor of lower provinces. It was classified as a rank for {{ill|Jige|ja|地下人}} (non-court nobles) and was distinctly separate from the Fifth Rank or higher, which denoted nobility ({{ill|Tsūki|ja|通貴}}). Those holding the Sixth Rank were not permitted {{ill|Shōden|ja|昇殿}} (entry to the palace). However, {{ill|Kurōdo|ja|蔵人}} (Chamberlains) were exceptions; their roles allowed them palace access, and those of the Sixth Rank were called {{ill|Rokui Kurōdo|ja|六位蔵人}}, collectively referred to as {{ill|Tenjōbito|ja|殿上人}} alongside those of the Fifth Rank or higher.
In terms of {{ill|Shinkai (divine rank)|lt=divine ranks|ja|神階|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}}, Shōrokui is the lowest rank. The {{ill|Samurai|ja|侍}} originated from the {{ill|Samuraihon|ja|侍品}} class, which consisted of individuals limited to the Sixth Rank who served as bureaucrats and aides to nobles. The Sixth Rank class was also referred to as {{ill|Aosamurai|ja|青侍}} (Blue Samurai) due to their {{ill|Po (clothing)|lt=Po robe|ja|袍|ja|袍}} being light blue in color.
In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}} and later, individuals holding ranks such as {{ill|Major|ja|少佐}} were appointed to this position.
Today, Shōrokui is posthumously conferred to individuals such as {{ill|Keishisei|ja|警視正}} (Senior Police Superintendent) in the {{ill|Japanese police|ja|日本の警察官}}, {{ill|Shōbōkan|ja|消防監}} (Fire Superintendent) in the {{ill|Firefighting personnel|ja|消防吏員}}, chairs of {{ill|Municipal councils in Japan|ja|日本の地方議会}} (city, town, and village councils), founders of special institutions and schools, and artists who have made significant contributions in their respective fields.
== External links == * [https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/document?lawid=215IO0000000325 位階令] - {{ill|e-Gov Law Search|ja|e-Gov法令検索}} * [{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 官位相当表] - National Diet Library Digital Archive

[[Category:new pages]] {{Citation needed|date=August 2015}}
'''Shōrokui''' (正六位) is one of the {{ill|Ranks of Japan|ja|位階}} and {{ill|Shinkai (divine rank)|lt=divine ranks|ja|神階|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位|1=Junior Fifth Rank}} and above {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank}}. In terms of decorations, it corresponds to the Fifth Class of {{ill|Orders of Merit|ja|勲等}} and the Fifth Class of the {{ill|Order of the Golden Kite|ja|金鵄勲章}}.
Under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, the Sixth Rank was granted to {{ill|Kaike|ja|介}}, who served as the deputy governor of lower provinces. It was classified as a rank for {{ill|Jige|ja|地下人}} (non-court nobles) and was distinctly separate from the Fifth Rank or higher, which denoted nobility ({{ill|Tsūki|ja|通貴}}). Those holding the Sixth Rank were not permitted {{ill|Shōden|ja|昇殿}} (entry to the palace). However, {{ill|Kurōdo|ja|蔵人}} (Chamberlains) were exceptions; their roles allowed them palace access, and those of the Sixth Rank were called {{ill|Rokui Kurōdo|ja|六位蔵人}}, collectively referred to as {{ill|Tenjōbito|ja|殿上人}} alongside those of the Fifth Rank or higher.

The {{ill|Samurai|ja|侍}} originated from the {{ill|Samuraihon|ja|侍品}} class, which consisted of individuals limited to the Sixth Rank who served as bureaucrats and aides to nobles. The Sixth Rank class was also referred to as {{ill|Aosamurai|ja|青侍}} (Blue Samurai) due to their {{ill|Po (clothing)|lt=Po robe|ja|袍|ja|袍}} being light blue in color.
In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}} and later, individuals holding ranks such as {{ill|Major|ja|少佐}} were appointed to this position.

revision.text:
[[Category:new pages]] {{Citation needed|date=August 2015}}
'''Shōrokui''' (正六位) is one of the {{ill|Ranks of Japan|ja|位階}} and {{ill|Shinkai (divine rank)|lt=divine ranks|ja|神階|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位|1=Junior Fifth Rank}} and above {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank}}. In terms of decorations, it corresponds to the Fifth Class of {{ill|Orders of Merit|ja|勲等}} and the Fifth Class of the {{ill|Order of the Golden Kite|ja|金鵄勲章}}.
Under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, the Sixth Rank was granted to {{ill|Kaike|ja|介}}, who served as the deputy governor of lower provinces. It was classified as a rank for {{ill|Jige|ja|地下人}} (non-court nobles) and was distinctly separate from the Fifth Rank or higher, which denoted nobility ({{ill|Tsūki|ja|通貴}}). Those holding the Sixth Rank were not permitted {{ill|Shōden|ja|昇殿}} (entry to the palace). However, {{ill|Kurōdo|ja|蔵人}} (Chamberlains) were exceptions; their roles allowed them palace access, and those of the Sixth Rank were called {{ill|Rokui Kurōdo|ja|六位蔵人}}, collectively referred to as {{ill|Tenjōbito|ja|殿上人}} alongside those of the Fifth Rank or higher.
In terms of {{ill|Shinkai (divine rank)|lt=divine ranks|ja|神階|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}}, Shōrokui is the lowest rank. The {{ill|Samurai|ja|侍}} originated from the {{ill|Samuraihon|ja|侍品}} class, which consisted of individuals limited to the Sixth Rank who served as bureaucrats and aides to nobles. The Sixth Rank class was also referred to as {{ill|Aosamurai|ja|青侍}} (Blue Samurai) due to their {{ill|Po (clothing)|lt=Po robe|ja|袍|ja|袍}} being light blue in color.
In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}} and later, individuals holding ranks such as {{ill|Major|ja|少佐}} were appointed to this position.
Today, Shōrokui is posthumously conferred to individuals such as {{ill|Keishisei|ja|警視正}} (Senior Police Superintendent) in the {{ill|Japanese police|ja|日本の警察官}}, {{ill|Shōbōkan|ja|消防監}} (Fire Superintendent) in the {{ill|Firefighting personnel|ja|消防吏員}}, chairs of {{ill|Municipal councils in Japan|ja|日本の地方議会}} (city, town, and village councils), founders of special institutions and schools, and artists who have made significant contributions in their respective fields.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "qqq" to "qq"
revision.text:
[[Category:new pages]] {{Citation needed|date=August 2015}}
'''Shōrokui''' (正六位) is one of the {{ill|Ranks of Japan|ja|位階}} and {{ill|Shinkai (divine rank)|lt=divine ranks|ja|神階|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位|1=Junior Fifth Rank}} and above {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank}}. In terms of decorations, it corresponds to the Fifth Class of {{ill|Orders of Merit|ja|勲等}} and the Fifth Class of the {{ill|Order of the Golden Kite|ja|金鵄勲章}}.
Under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, the Sixth Rank was granted to {{ill|Kaike|ja|介}}, who served as the deputy governor of lower provinces. It was classified as a rank for {{ill|Jige|ja|地下人}} (non-court nobles) and was distinctly separate from the Fifth Rank or higher, which denoted nobility ({{ill|Tsūki|ja|通貴}}). Those holding the Sixth Rank were not permitted {{ill|Shōden|ja|昇殿}} (entry to the palace). However, {{ill|Kurōdo|ja|蔵人}} (Chamberlains) were exceptions; their roles allowed them palace access, and those of the Sixth Rank were called {{ill|Rokui Kurōdo|ja|六位蔵人}}, collectively referred to as {{ill|Tenjōbito|ja|殿上人}} alongside those of the Fifth Rank or higher.
In terms of {{ill|Shinkai (divine rank)|lt=divine ranks|ja|神階|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}}, Shōrokui is the lowest rank. The {{ill|Samurai|ja|侍}} originated from the {{ill|Samuraihon|ja|侍品}} class, which consisted of individuals limited to the Sixth Rank who served as bureaucrats and aides to nobles. The Sixth Rank class was also referred to as {{ill|Aosamurai|ja|青侍}} (Blue Samurai) due to their {{ill|Po (clothing)|lt=Po robe|ja|袍|ja|袍}} being light blue in color.
In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}} and later, individuals holding ranks such as {{ill|Major|ja|少佐}} were appointed to this position.
Today, Shōrokui is posthumously conferred to individuals such as {{ill|Keishisei|ja|警視正}} (Senior Police Superintendent) in the {{ill|Japanese police|ja|日本の警察官}}, {{ill|Shōbōkan|ja|消防監}} (Fire Superintendent) in the {{ill|Firefighting personnel|ja|消防吏員}}, chairs of {{ill|Municipal councils in Japan|ja|日本の地方議会}} (city, town, and village councils), founders of special institutions and schools, and artists who have made significant contributions in their respective fields.

revision.text:
[[Category:Completion tier 2]] {{Citation needed|date=August 2015}}
'''Shōrokui''' (正六位) is one of the {{ill|Ranks of Japan|ja|位階}} and {{ill|Shinkai (divine rank)|lt=divine ranks|ja|神階|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位|1=Junior Fifth Rank}} and above {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank}}. In terms of decorations, it corresponds to the Fifth Class of {{ill|Orders of Merit|ja|勲等}} and the Fifth Class of the {{ill|Order of the Golden Kite|ja|金鵄勲章}}.
Under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, the Sixth Rank was granted to {{ill|Kaike|ja|介}}, who served as the deputy governor of lower provinces. It was classified as a rank for {{ill|Jige|ja|地下人}} (non-court nobles) and was distinctly separate from the Fifth Rank or higher, which denoted nobility ({{ill|Tsūki|ja|通貴}}). Those holding the Sixth Rank were not permitted {{ill|Shōden|ja|昇殿}} (entry to the palace). However, {{ill|Kurōdo|ja|蔵人}} (Chamberlains) were exceptions; their roles allowed them palace access, and those of the Sixth Rank were called {{ill|Rokui Kurōdo|ja|六位蔵人}}, collectively referred to as {{ill|Tenjōbito|ja|殿上人}} alongside those of the Fifth Rank or higher.
In terms of {{ill|Shinkai (divine rank)|lt=divine ranks|ja|神階|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}}, Shōrokui is the lowest rank. The {{ill|Samurai|ja|侍}} originated from the {{ill|Samuraihon|ja|侍品}} class, which consisted of individuals limited to the Sixth Rank who served as bureaucrats and aides to nobles. The Sixth Rank class was also referred to as {{ill|Aosamurai|ja|青侍}} (Blue Samurai) due to their {{ill|Po (clothing)|lt=Po robe|ja|袍|ja|袍}} being light blue in color.
In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}} and later, individuals holding ranks such as {{ill|Major|ja|少佐}} were appointed to this position.
Today, Shōrokui is posthumously conferred to individuals such as {{ill|Keishisei|ja|警視正}} (Senior Police Superintendent) in the {{ill|Japanese police|ja|日本の警察官}}, {{ill|Shōbōkan|ja|消防監}} (Fire Superintendent) in the {{ill|Firefighting personnel|ja|消防吏員}}, chairs of {{ill|Municipal councils in Japan|ja|日本の地方議会}} (city, town, and village councils), founders of special institutions and schools, and artists who have made significant contributions in their respective fields.
== External links == * [https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/document?lawid=215IO0000000325 位階令] - {{ill|e-Gov Law Search|ja|e-Gov法令検索}} * [{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 官位相当表] - National Diet Library Digital Archive

revision.text:
[[Category:Completion tier 3]] {{Citation needed|date=August 2015}}
'''Shōrokui''' (正六位) is one of the {{ill|Ranks of Japan|ja|位階}} and {{ill|Shinkai (divine rank)|lt=divine ranks|ja|神階|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位|1=Junior Fifth Rank}} and above {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank}}. In terms of decorations, it corresponds to the Fifth Class of {{ill|Orders of Merit|ja|勲等}} and the Fifth Class of the {{ill|Order of the Golden Kite|ja|金鵄勲章}}.
Under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, the Sixth Rank was granted to {{ill|Kaike|ja|介}}, who served as the deputy governor of lower provinces. It was classified as a rank for {{ill|Jige|ja|地下人}} (non-court nobles) and was distinctly separate from the Fifth Rank or higher, which denoted nobility ({{ill|Tsūki|ja|通貴}}). Those holding the Sixth Rank were not permitted {{ill|Shōden|ja|昇殿}} (entry to the palace). However, {{ill|Kurōdo|ja|蔵人}} (Chamberlains) were exceptions; their roles allowed them palace access, and those of the Sixth Rank were called {{ill|Rokui Kurōdo|ja|六位蔵人}}, collectively referred to as {{ill|Tenjōbito|ja|殿上人}} alongside those of the Fifth Rank or higher.
In terms of {{ill|Shinkai (divine rank)|lt=divine ranks|ja|神階|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}}, Shōrokui is the lowest rank. The {{ill|Samurai|ja|侍}} originated from the {{ill|Samuraihon|ja|侍品}} class, which consisted of individuals limited to the Sixth Rank who served as bureaucrats and aides to nobles. The Sixth Rank class was also referred to as {{ill|Aosamurai|ja|青侍}} (Blue Samurai) due to their {{ill|Po (clothing)|lt=Po robe|ja|袍|ja|袍}} being light blue in color.
In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}} and later, individuals holding ranks such as {{ill|Major|ja|少佐}} were appointed to this position.
Today, Shōrokui is posthumously conferred to individuals such as {{ill|Keishisei|ja|警視正}} (Senior Police Superintendent) in the {{ill|Japanese police|ja|日本の警察官}}, {{ill|Shōbōkan|ja|消防監}} (Fire Superintendent) in the {{ill|Firefighting personnel|ja|消防吏員}}, chairs of {{ill|Municipal councils in Japan|ja|日本の地方議会}} (city, town, and village councils), founders of special institutions and schools, and artists who have made significant contributions in their respective fields.
== External links == * [https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/document?lawid=215IO0000000325 位階令] - {{ill|e-Gov Law Search|ja|e-Gov法令検索}} * [{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 官位相当表] - National Diet Library Digital Archive

[[Category:Court Ranks of Japan]][[Category:completion tier 4]] {{Citation needed|date=August 2015}}
'''Shōrokui''' (正六位) is one of the {{ill|Ranks of Japan|ja|位階}} and {{ill|Shinkai (divine rank)|lt=divine ranks|ja|神階|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位|1=Junior Fifth Rank}} and above {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank}}.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-ranks/court-ranks/</ref> In terms of decorations, it corresponds to the Fifth Class of {{ill|Orders of Merit|ja|勲等}} and the Fifth Class of the {{ill|Order of the Golden Kite|ja|金鵄勲章}}.
Under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, the Sixth Rank was granted to {{ill|Kaike|ja|介}}, who served as the deputy governor of lower provinces. It was classified as a rank for {{ill|Jige|ja|地下人}} (non-court nobles) and was distinctly separate from the Fifth Rank or higher, which denoted nobility ({{ill|Tsūki|ja|通貴}}). Those holding the Sixth Rank were not permitted {{ill|Shōden|ja|昇殿}} (entry to the palace). However, {{ill|Kurōdo|ja|蔵人}} (Chamberlains) were exceptions; their roles allowed them palace access, and those of the Sixth Rank were called {{ill|Rokui Kurōdo|ja|六位蔵人}}, collectively referred to as {{ill|Tenjōbito|ja|殿上人}} alongside those of the Fifth Rank or higher.
In terms of {{ill|Shinkai (divine rank)|lt=divine ranks|ja|神階|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}}, Shōrokui is the lowest rank. The {{ill|Samurai|ja|侍}} originated from the {{ill|Samuraihon|ja|侍品}} class, which consisted of individuals limited to the Sixth Rank who served as bureaucrats and aides to nobles. The Sixth Rank class was also referred to as {{ill|Aosamurai|ja|青侍}} (Blue Samurai) due to their {{ill|Po (clothing)|lt=Po robe|ja|袍|ja|袍}} being light blue in color.
In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}} and later, individuals holding ranks such as {{ill|Major|ja|少佐}} were appointed to this position.
Today, Shōrokui is posthumously conferred to individuals such as {{ill|Keishisei|ja|警視正}} (Senior Police Superintendent) in the {{ill|Japanese police|ja|日本の警察官}}, {{ill|Shōbōkan|ja|消防監}} (Fire Superintendent) in the {{ill|Firefighting personnel|ja|消防吏員}}, chairs of {{ill|Municipal councils in Japan|ja|日本の地方議会}} (city, town, and village councils), founders of special institutions and schools, and artists who have made significant contributions in their respective fields.

revision.comment: Bot: Reformatted page - moved categories, interwikis, DEFAULTSORT, and removed duplicates.
revision.text:
{{Citation needed|date=August 2015}}
'''Shōrokui''' (正六位) is one of the {{ill|Ranks of Japan|ja|位階}} and {{ill|Shinkai (divine rank)|lt=divine ranks|ja|神階|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位|1=Junior Fifth Rank}} and above {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank}}.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-ranks/court-ranks/</ref> In terms of decorations, it corresponds to the Fifth Class of {{ill|Orders of Merit|ja|勲等}} and the Fifth Class of the {{ill|Order of the Golden Kite|ja|金鵄勲章}}.
Under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, the Sixth Rank was granted to {{ill|Kaike|ja|介}}, who served as the deputy governor of lower provinces. It was classified as a rank for {{ill|Jige|ja|地下人}} (non-court nobles) and was distinctly separate from the Fifth Rank or higher, which denoted nobility ({{ill|Tsūki|ja|通貴}}). Those holding the Sixth Rank were not permitted {{ill|Shōden|ja|昇殿}} (entry to the palace). However, {{ill|Kurōdo|ja|蔵人}} (Chamberlains) were exceptions; their roles allowed them palace access, and those of the Sixth Rank were called {{ill|Rokui Kurōdo|ja|六位蔵人}}, collectively referred to as {{ill|Tenjōbito|ja|殿上人}} alongside those of the Fifth Rank or higher.
In terms of {{ill|Shinkai (divine rank)|lt=divine ranks|ja|神階|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}}, Shōrokui is the lowest rank. The {{ill|Samurai|ja|侍}} originated from the {{ill|Samuraihon|ja|侍品}} class, which consisted of individuals limited to the Sixth Rank who served as bureaucrats and aides to nobles. The Sixth Rank class was also referred to as {{ill|Aosamurai|ja|青侍}} (Blue Samurai) due to their {{ill|Po (clothing)|lt=Po robe|ja|袍|ja|袍}} being light blue in color.
In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}} and later, individuals holding ranks such as {{ill|Major|ja|少佐}} were appointed to this position.
Today, Shōrokui is posthumously conferred to individuals such as {{ill|Keishisei|ja|警視正}} (Senior Police Superintendent) in the {{ill|Japanese police|ja|日本の警察官}}, {{ill|Shōbōkan|ja|消防監}} (Fire Superintendent) in the {{ill|Firefighting personnel|ja|消防吏員}}, chairs of {{ill|Municipal councils in Japan|ja|日本の地方議会}} (city, town, and village councils), founders of special institutions and schools, and artists who have made significant contributions in their respective fields.
== External links == * [https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/document?lawid=215IO0000000325 位階令] - {{ill|e-Gov Law Search|ja|e-Gov法令検索}} * [{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 官位相当表] - National Diet Library Digital Archive

Today, Shōrokui is posthumously conferred to individuals such as {{ill|Keishisei|ja|警視正|12=draft}} (Senior Police Superintendent) in the {{ill|Japanese police|ja|日本の警察官|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shōbōkan|ja|消防監|12=draft}} (Fire Superintendent) in the {{ill|Firefighting personnel|ja|消防吏員|12=draft}}, chairs of {{ill|Municipal councils in Japan|ja|日本の地方議会|12=draft}} (city, town, and village councils), founders of special institutions and schools, and artists who have made significant contributions in their respective fields.
== External links == * [https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/document?lawid=215IO0000000325 位階令] - {{ill|e-Gov Law Search|ja|e-Gov法令検索|12=draft}} * [{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 官位相当表] - National Diet Library Digital Archive <!--Senior Sixth Rank--><!--Senior Sixth Rank--><!--Senior Sixth Rank-->

{{draft categories| [[Category:Court Ranks of Japan]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:original templates]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:正六位]] [[sn:Senior Sixth Rank]] [[sn:enior Sixth Rank]]

{{出典の明記|date=2015年8月}} '''正六位'''（しょうろくい）とは、[[日本]]の[[位階]]及び[[神階]]における位のひとつ。[[従五位]]の下、[[従六位]]の上に位する。[[勲等]]では勲五等に、[[金鵄勲章|功級]]では功五級に相当。
[[律令制]]下において六位は下国の[[国司]]及び[[国府]]の次官である介が叙せられる位であった。[[地下人]]の位階とされ、五位以上の[[貴族]]（通貴）とは一線を画する位階であり[[昇殿]]は許されなかった。ただし、[[蔵人]]の場合、その職務上、六位であっても昇殿が許され、五位以上の者と[[六位蔵人]]の者を合わせて[[殿上人]]と称した。神階においては、正六位が最下位となる。[[侍]]は朝廷の実務を担い貴族や諸大夫に仕える、位階六位どまりの侍品（さむらいほん）が元である。六位層は[[袍]]が[[縹]]色であることから、後には[[青侍]]と呼ばれた。
[[明治時代]]以降は、[[少佐]]の階級にある者などがこの位に叙せられた。
現在、[[日本の警察官|警察官]]では[[警視正]]、[[消防吏員]]では[[消防監]]などが叙せられるほか、[[日本の地方議会|市町村議会]][[議長]]にあった者、特別施設や学校の創立者、あるいは芸術家で功労ある者などが没後に叙せられる。
==外部リンク== * [https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/law/215IO0000000325 位階令] - [[e-Gov法令検索]] * [{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 官位相当表] - 国立国会図書館近代デジタルライブラリー
{{日本の位階}} {{デフォルトソート:しようろくい}} [[Category:位階制度|しよう6い]]

revision.text:
{{Citation needed|date=August 2015}}
'''Shōrokui''' (正六位) is one of the {{ill|Ranks of Japan|ja|位階|12=draft}} and {{ill|Shinkai (divine rank)|lt=divine ranks|ja|神階|1=Shinkai (divine rank)|12=draft}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位|1=Junior Fifth Rank|12=draft}} and above {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank|12=draft}}.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-ranks/court-ranks/</ref> In terms of decorations, it corresponds to the Fifth Class of {{ill|Orders of Merit|ja|勲等|12=draft}} and the Fifth Class of the {{ill|Order of the Golden Kite|ja|金鵄勲章|12=draft}}.
Under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制|12=draft}}, the Sixth Rank was granted to {{ill|Kaike|ja|介|12=draft}}, who served as the deputy governor of lower provinces. It was classified as a rank for {{ill|Jige|ja|地下人|12=draft}} (non-court nobles) and was distinctly separate from the Fifth Rank or higher, which denoted nobility ({{ill|Tsūki|ja|通貴|12=draft}}). Those holding the Sixth Rank were not permitted {{ill|Shōden|ja|昇殿|12=draft}} (entry to the palace). However, {{ill|Kurōdo|ja|蔵人|12=draft}} (Chamberlains) were exceptions; their roles allowed them palace access, and those of the Sixth Rank were called {{ill|Rokui Kurōdo|ja|六位蔵人|12=draft}}, collectively referred to as {{ill|Tenjōbito|ja|殿上人|12=draft}} alongside those of the Fifth Rank or higher.
In terms of {{ill|Shinkai (divine rank)|lt=divine ranks|ja|神階|1=Shinkai (divine rank)|12=draft}}, Shōrokui is the lowest rank. The {{ill|Samurai|ja|侍|12=draft}} originated from the {{ill|Samuraihon|ja|侍品|12=draft}} class, which consisted of individuals limited to the Sixth Rank who served as bureaucrats and aides to nobles. The Sixth Rank class was also referred to as {{ill|Aosamurai|ja|青侍|12=draft}} (Blue Samurai) due to their {{ill|Po (clothing)|lt=Po robe|ja|袍|ja|袍|12=draft}} being light blue in color.
In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代|12=draft}} and later, individuals holding ranks such as {{ill|Major|ja|少佐|12=draft}} were appointed to this position.
Today, Shōrokui is posthumously conferred to individuals such as {{ill|Keishisei|ja|警視正|12=draft}} (Senior Police Superintendent) in the {{ill|Japanese police|ja|日本の警察官|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shōbōkan|ja|消防監|12=draft}} (Fire Superintendent) in the {{ill|Firefighting personnel|ja|消防吏員|12=draft}}, chairs of {{ill|Municipal councils in Japan|ja|日本の地方議会|12=draft}} (city, town, and village councils), founders of special institutions and schools, and artists who have made significant contributions in their respective fields.
== External links == * [https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/document?lawid=215IO0000000325 位階令] - {{ill|e-Gov Law Search|ja|e-Gov法令検索|12=draft}} * [{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 官位相当表] - National Diet Library Digital Archive <!--Senior Sixth Rank--><!--Senior Sixth Rank--><!--Senior Sixth Rank-->
<!--Senior Sixth Rank--><!--Senior Sixth Rank--><!--Senior Sixth Rank-->
<!--Senior Sixth Rank--><!--Senior Sixth Rank--><!--Senior Sixth Rank--><!--Senior Sixth Rank--><!--Senior Sixth Rank--><!--Senior Sixth Rank--><!--Senior Sixth Rank-->

The Sixth Rank class was also referred to as {{ill|Aosamurai|ja|青侍|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Blue Samurai) due to their {{ill|Po (clothing)|lt=Po robe|ja|袍|ja|袍|qq=draft|qq=draft}} being light blue in color.
In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and later, individuals holding Ranks such as {{ill|Major|ja|少佐|qq=draft|qq=draft}} were appointed to this position.
Today, Shōrokui is posthumously conferred to individuals such as {{ill|Keishisei|ja|警視正|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Senior Police Superintendent) in the {{ill|Japanese police|ja|日本の警察官|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Shōbōkan|ja|消防監|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Fire Superintendent) in the {{ill|Firefighting personnel|ja|消防吏員|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, chairs of {{ill|Municipal councils in Japan|ja|日本の地方議会|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (city, town, and village councils), founders of special institutions and schools, and artists who have made significant contributions in their respective fields.

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Citation needed|date=August 2015}}
'''Shōrokui''' (正六位) is one of the {{ill|Ranks of Japan|ja|位階|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Shinkai (divine rank)|lt=divine Ranks|ja|神階|1=Shinkai (divine rank)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位|1=Junior Fifth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and above {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-Ranks/court-Ranks/</ref> In terms of decorations, it corresponds to the Fifth Class of {{ill|Orders of Merit|ja|勲等|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the Fifth Class of the {{ill|Order of the Golden Kite|ja|金鵄勲章|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
Under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the Sixth Rank was granted to {{ill|Kaike|ja|介|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who served as the deputy governor of lower provinces. It was classified as a Rank for {{ill|Jige|ja|地下人|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (non-court nobles) and was distinctly separate from the Fifth Rank or higher, which denoted nobility ({{ill|Tsūki|ja|通貴|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). Those holding the Sixth Rank were not permitted {{ill|Shōden|ja|昇殿|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (entry to the palace). However, {{ill|Kurōdo|ja|蔵人|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Chamberlains) were exceptions; their roles allowed them palace access, and those of the Sixth Rank were called {{ill|Rokui Kurōdo|ja|六位蔵人|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, collectively referred to as {{ill|Tenjōbito|ja|殿上人|qq=draft|qq=draft}} alongside those of the Fifth Rank or higher.
In terms of {{ill|Shinkai (divine rank)|lt=divine Ranks|ja|神階|1=Shinkai (divine rank)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Shōrokui is the lowest Rank. The {{ill|Samurai|ja|侍|qq=draft|qq=draft}} originated from the {{ill|Samuraihon|ja|侍品|qq=draft|qq=draft}} class, which consisted of individuals limited to the Sixth Rank who served as bureaucrats and aides to nobles. The Sixth Rank class was also referred to as {{ill|Aosamurai|ja|青侍|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Blue Samurai) due to their {{ill|Po (clothing)|lt=Po robe|ja|袍|ja|袍|qq=draft|qq=draft}} being light blue in color.
In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and later, individuals holding Ranks such as {{ill|Major|ja|少佐|qq=draft|qq=draft}} were appointed to this position.
Today, Shōrokui is posthumously conferred to individuals such as {{ill|Keishisei|ja|警視正|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Senior Police Superintendent) in the {{ill|Japanese police|ja|日本の警察官|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Shōbōkan|ja|消防監|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Fire Superintendent) in the {{ill|Firefighting personnel|ja|消防吏員|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, chairs of {{ill|Municipal councils in Japan|ja|日本の地方議会|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (city, town, and village councils), founders of special institutions and schools, and artists who have made significant contributions in their respective fields.
== External links == * [https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/document?lawid=215IO0000000325 位階令] - {{ill|e-Gov Law Search|ja|e-Gov法令検索|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * [{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 官位相当表] - National Diet Library Digital Archive
{{Court Ranks of Japan}} *

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11545372]]
Wikidata name: Senior Sixth Rank
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Citation needed|date=August 2015}}

'''Shōrokui''' (正六位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks in Japan|ja|位階|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ranks of Japan|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and [[:en:Shinkai (divine rank)|divine Ranks]]. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位|1=Junior Fifth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} and above {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|qq=}}.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-Ranks/court-Ranks/</ref> In terms of decorations, it corresponds to the Fifth Class of {{ill|Orders of Merit|ja|勲等|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Orders of Merit|qq=|1=Japanese honors system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17990718}} and the Fifth Class of the [[:en:Order of the Golden Kite|Order of the Golden Kite]].

Under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], the Sixth Rank was granted to {{ill|Kaike|ja|介|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaike|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国司|1=kokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1351234}}, who served as the deputy governor of lower provinces. It was classified as a Rank for {{ill|Jige|ja|地下人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jige|qq=|1=jige officials|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424047}} (non-court nobles) and was distinctly separate from the Fifth Rank or higher, which denoted nobility ({{ill|Tsūki|ja|通貴|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsūki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). Those holding the Sixth Rank were not permitted {{ill|Shōden|ja|昇殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōden|qq=|1=Shōden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11511916}} (entry to the palace). However, [[:en:Kurōdo-dokoro|Kurōdo]] (Chamberlains) were exceptions; their roles allowed them palace access, and those of the Sixth Rank were called {{ill|Rokui Kurōdo|ja|六位蔵人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokui Kurōdo|qq=|1=Rokui Kurōdo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392278}}, collectively referred to as {{ill|Tenjōbito|ja|殿上人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjōbito|qq=|1=court nobles|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10369708}} alongside those of the Fifth Rank or higher.

In terms of [[:en:Shinkai (divine rank)|divine Ranks]], Shōrokui is the lowest Rank. The [[:en:Samurai|Samurai]] originated from the {{ill|Samuraihon|ja|侍品|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuraihon|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} class, which consisted of individuals limited to the Sixth Rank who served as bureaucrats and aides to nobles. The Sixth Rank class was also referred to as {{ill|Aosamurai|ja|青侍|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aosamurai|qq=}} (Blue Samurai) due to their {{ill|Po (clothing)|lt=Po robe|ja|袍|ja|袍|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Po (clothing)|qq=|1=hō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134436881}} being light blue in color.

In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]] and later, individuals holding Ranks such as [[:en:Major (rank)|Major]] were appointed to this position.

Today, Shōrokui is posthumously conferred to individuals such as {{ill|Keishisei|ja|警視正|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keishisei|qq=|1=Assistant Commissioner|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632424}} (Senior Police Superintendent) in the {{ill|Japanese police|ja|日本の警察官|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese police|qq=|1=Japanese police officer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11506095}}, {{ill|Shōbōkan|ja|消防監|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōbōkan|qq=}} (Fire Superintendent) in the {{ill|Firefighting personnel|ja|消防吏員|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Firefighting personnel|qq=|1=Uniformed firefighter in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1255655}}, chairs of {{ill|Municipal councils in Japan|ja|日本の地方議会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Municipal councils in Japan|qq=|1=local assembly in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505876}} (city, town, and village councils), founders of special institutions and schools, and artists who have made significant contributions in their respective fields.

Those holding the Sixth Rank were not permitted {{ill|Shōden|ja|昇殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōden|qq=|1=Shōden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11511916}} (entry to the palace). However, [[:en:Kurōdo-dokoro|Kurōdo]] (Chamberlains) were exceptions; their roles allowed them palace access, and those of the Sixth Rank were called {{ill|Rokui Kurōdo|ja|六位蔵人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokui Kurōdo|qq=|1=Rokui Kurōdo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392278}}, collectively referred to as {{ill|Tenjōbito|ja|殿上人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjōbito|qq=|1=court nobles|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10369708}} alongside those of the Fifth Rank or higher.

In terms of [[:en:Shinkai (divine rank)|divine Ranks]], Shōrokui is the lowest Rank. The [[:en:Samurai|Samurai]] originated from the {{ill|Samuraihon|ja|侍品|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuraihon|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} class, which consisted of individuals limited to the Sixth Rank who served as bureaucrats and aides to nobles. The Sixth Rank class was also referred to as {{ill|Aosamurai|ja|青侍|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aosamurai|qq=}} (Blue Samurai) due to their {{ill|Po (clothing)|lt=Po robe|ja|袍|ja|袍|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Po (clothing)|qq=|1=hō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134436881}} being light blue in color.

In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]] and later, individuals holding Ranks such as [[:en:Major (rank)|Major]] were appointed to this position.

Today, Shōrokui is posthumously conferred to individuals such as {{ill|Keishisei|ja|警視正|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keishisei|qq=|1=Assistant Commissioner|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632424}} (Senior Police Superintendent) in the {{ill|Japanese police|ja|日本の警察官|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese police|qq=|1=Japanese police officer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11506095}}, {{ill|Shōbōkan|ja|消防監|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōbōkan|qq=}} (Fire Superintendent) in the {{ill|Firefighting personnel|ja|消防吏員|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Firefighting personnel|qq=|1=Uniformed firefighter in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1255655}}, chairs of {{ill|Municipal councils in Japan|ja|日本の地方議会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Municipal councils in Japan|qq=|1=local assembly in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505876}} (city, town, and village councils), founders of special institutions and schools, and artists who have made significant contributions in their respective fields.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:正六位]][[ko:정6위]][[zh:正六位]]
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11545372]]
Wikidata name: Senior Sixth Rank
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Citation needed|date=August 2015}}

'''Shōrokui''' (正六位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks in Japan|ja|位階|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ranks of Japan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and [[:en:Shinkai (divine rank)|divine Ranks]]. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位|1=Junior Fifth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} and above {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|qq=}}.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-Ranks/court-Ranks/</ref> In terms of decorations, it corresponds to the Fifth Class of {{ill|Orders of Merit|ja|勲等|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Orders of Merit|1=Japanese honors system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17990718}} and the Fifth Class of the [[:en:Order of the Golden Kite|Order of the Golden Kite]].

Under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], the Sixth Rank was granted to {{ill|Kaike|ja|介|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaike|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国司|1=kokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1351234}}, who served as the deputy governor of lower provinces. It was classified as a Rank for {{ill|Jige|ja|地下人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jige|1=jige officials|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424047}} (non-court nobles) and was distinctly separate from the Fifth Rank or higher, which denoted nobility ({{ill|Tsūki|ja|通貴|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsūki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). Those holding the Sixth Rank were not permitted {{ill|Shōden|ja|昇殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōden|1=Shōden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11511916}} (entry to the palace). However, [[:en:Kurōdo-dokoro|Kurōdo]] (Chamberlains) were exceptions; their roles allowed them palace access, and those of the Sixth Rank were called {{ill|Rokui Kurōdo|ja|六位蔵人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokui Kurōdo|1=Rokui Kurōdo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392278}}, collectively referred to as {{ill|Tenjōbito|ja|殿上人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjōbito|1=court nobles|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10369708}} alongside those of the Fifth Rank or higher.

In terms of [[:en:Shinkai (divine rank)|divine Ranks]], Shōrokui is the lowest Rank. The [[:en:Samurai|Samurai]] originated from the {{ill|Samuraihon|ja|侍品|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuraihon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} class, which consisted of individuals limited to the Sixth Rank who served as bureaucrats and aides to nobles. The Sixth Rank class was also referred to as {{ill|Aosamurai|ja|青侍|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aosamurai|qq=}} (Blue Samurai) due to their {{ill|Po (clothing)|lt=Po robe|ja|袍|ja|袍|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Po (clothing)|1=hō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134436881}} being light blue in color.

In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]] and later, individuals holding Ranks such as [[:en:Major (rank)|Major]] were appointed to this position.

Today, Shōrokui is posthumously conferred to individuals such as {{ill|Keishisei|ja|警視正|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keishisei|1=Assistant Commissioner|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632424}} (Senior Police Superintendent) in the {{ill|Japanese police|ja|日本の警察官|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese police|1=Japanese police officer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11506095}}, {{ill|Shōbōkan|ja|消防監|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōbōkan|qq=}} (Fire Superintendent) in the {{ill|Firefighting personnel|ja|消防吏員|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Firefighting personnel|1=Uniformed firefighter in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1255655}}, chairs of {{ill|Municipal councils in Japan|ja|日本の地方議会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Municipal councils in Japan|1=local assembly in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505876}} (city, town, and village councils), founders of special institutions and schools, and artists who have made significant contributions in their respective fields.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:正六位]][[ko:정6위]][[zh:正六位]]
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11545372]]
Wikidata name: Senior Sixth Rank
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Citation needed|date=August 2015}}

'''Shōrokui''' (正六位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks in Japan|ja|位階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ranks of Japan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and [[:en:Shinkai (divine rank)|divine Ranks]]. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位|1=Junior Fifth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} and above {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|qq=}}.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-Ranks/court-Ranks/</ref> In terms of decorations, it corresponds to the Fifth Class of {{ill|Orders of Merit|ja|勲等|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Orders of Merit|1=Japanese honors system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17990718}} and the Fifth Class of the [[:en:Order of the Golden Kite|Order of the Golden Kite]].

Under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], the Sixth Rank was granted to {{ill|Kaike|ja|介|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaike|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国司|1=kokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1351234}}, who served as the deputy governor of lower provinces. It was classified as a Rank for {{ill|Jige|ja|地下人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jige|1=jige officials|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424047}} (non-court nobles) and was distinctly separate from the Fifth Rank or higher, which denoted nobility ({{ill|Tsūki|ja|通貴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsūki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). Those holding the Sixth Rank were not permitted {{ill|Shōden|ja|昇殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōden|1=Shōden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11511916}} (entry to the palace). However, [[:en:Kurōdo-dokoro|Kurōdo]] (Chamberlains) were exceptions; their roles allowed them palace access, and those of the Sixth Rank were called {{ill|Rokui Kurōdo|ja|六位蔵人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokui Kurōdo|1=Rokui Kurōdo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392278}}, collectively referred to as {{ill|Tenjōbito|ja|殿上人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjōbito|1=court nobles|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10369708}} alongside those of the Fifth Rank or higher.

In terms of [[:en:Shinkai (divine rank)|divine Ranks]], Shōrokui is the lowest Rank. The [[:en:Samurai|Samurai]] originated from the {{ill|Samuraihon|ja|侍品|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuraihon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} class, which consisted of individuals limited to the Sixth Rank who served as bureaucrats and aides to nobles. The Sixth Rank class was also referred to as {{ill|Aosamurai|ja|青侍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aosamurai|qq=}} (Blue Samurai) due to their {{ill|Po (clothing)|lt=Po robe|ja|袍|ja|袍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Po (clothing)|1=hō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134436881}} being light blue in color.

In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]] and later, individuals holding Ranks such as [[:en:Major (rank)|Major]] were appointed to this position.

Today, Shōrokui is posthumously conferred to individuals such as {{ill|Keishisei|ja|警視正|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keishisei|1=Assistant Commissioner|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632424}} (Senior Police Superintendent) in the {{ill|Japanese police|ja|日本の警察官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese police|1=Japanese police officer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11506095}}, {{ill|Shōbōkan|ja|消防監|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōbōkan|qq=}} (Fire Superintendent) in the {{ill|Firefighting personnel|ja|消防吏員|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Firefighting personnel|1=Uniformed firefighter in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1255655}}, chairs of {{ill|Municipal councils in Japan|ja|日本の地方議会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Municipal councils in Japan|1=local assembly in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505876}} (city, town, and village councils), founders of special institutions and schools, and artists who have made significant contributions in their respective fields.

'''Shōrokui''' (正六位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks in Japan|ja|位階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ranks of Japan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and [[:en:Shinkai (divine rank)|divine Ranks]]. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位|1=Junior Fifth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} and above {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|qq=}}.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-Ranks/court-Ranks/</ref> In terms of decorations, it corresponds to the Fifth Class of {{ill|Orders of Merit|ja|勲等|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Orders of Merit|1=Japanese honors system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17990718}} and the Fifth Class of the [[:en:Order of the Golden Kite|Order of the Golden Kite]].
Under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], the Sixth Rank was granted to {{ill|Kaike|ja|介|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaike|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国司|1=kokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1351234}}, who served as the deputy governor of lower provinces. It was classified as a Rank for {{ill|Jige|ja|地下人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jige|1=jige officials|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424047}} (non-court nobles) and was distinctly separate from the Fifth Rank or higher, which denoted nobility ({{ill|Tsūki|ja|通貴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsūki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). Those holding the Sixth Rank were not permitted {{ill|Shōden|ja|昇殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōden|1=Shōden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11511916}} (entry to the palace). However, [[:en:Kurōdo-dokoro|Kurōdo]] (Chamberlains) were exceptions; their roles allowed them palace access, and those of the Sixth Rank were called {{ill|Rokui Kurōdo|ja|六位蔵人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokui Kurōdo|1=Rokui Kurōdo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392278}}, collectively referred to as {{ill|Tenjōbito|ja|殿上人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjōbito|1=court nobles|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10369708}} alongside those of the Fifth Rank or higher.
In terms of [[:en:Shinkai (divine rank)|divine Ranks]], Shōrokui is the lowest Rank. The [[:en:Samurai|Samurai]] originated from the {{ill|Samuraihon|ja|侍品|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuraihon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} class, which consisted of individuals limited to the Sixth Rank who served as bureaucrats and aides to nobles. The Sixth Rank class was also referred to as {{ill|Aosamurai|ja|青侍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aosamurai|qq=}} (Blue Samurai) due to their {{ill|Po (clothing)|lt=Po robe|ja|袍|ja|袍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Po (clothing)|1=hō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134436881}} being light blue in color.
In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]] and later, individuals holding Ranks such as [[:en:Major (rank)|Major]] were appointed to this position.
Today, Shōrokui is posthumously conferred to individuals such as {{ill|Keishisei|ja|警視正|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keishisei|1=Assistant Commissioner|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632424}} (Senior Police Superintendent) in the {{ill|Japanese police|ja|日本の警察官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese police|1=Japanese police officer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11506095}}, {{ill|Shōbōkan|ja|消防監|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōbōkan|qq=}} (Fire Superintendent) in the {{ill|Firefighting personnel|ja|消防吏員|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Firefighting personnel|1=Uniformed firefighter in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1255655}}, chairs of {{ill|Municipal councils in Japan|ja|日本の地方議会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Municipal councils in Japan|1=local assembly in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505876}} (city, town, and village councils), founders of special institutions and schools, and artists who have made significant contributions in their respective fields.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:正六位]][[ko:정6위]][[zh:正六位]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11545372]] Wikidata name: Senior Sixth Rank [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Citation needed|date=August 2015}}
'''Shōrokui''' (正六位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks in Japan|ja|位階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ranks of Japan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and [[:en:Shinkai (divine rank)|divine Ranks]]. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位|1=Junior Fifth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} and above {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|qq=}}.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-Ranks/court-Ranks/</ref> In terms of decorations, it corresponds to the Fifth Class of {{ill|Orders of Merit|ja|勲等|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Orders of Merit|1=Japanese honors system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17990718}} and the Fifth Class of the [[:en:Order of the Golden Kite|Order of the Golden Kite]].
Under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], the Sixth Rank was granted to {{ill|Kaike|ja|介|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaike|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国司|1=kokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1351234}}, who served as the deputy governor of lower provinces. It was classified as a Rank for {{ill|Jige|ja|地下人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jige|1=jige officials|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424047}} (non-court nobles) and was distinctly separate from the Fifth Rank or higher, which denoted nobility ({{ill|Tsūki|ja|通貴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsūki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). Those holding the Sixth Rank were not permitted {{ill|Shōden|ja|昇殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōden|1=Shōden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11511916}} (entry to the palace). However, [[:en:Kurōdo-dokoro|Kurōdo]] (Chamberlains) were exceptions; their roles allowed them palace access, and those of the Sixth Rank were called {{ill|Rokui Kurōdo|ja|六位蔵人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokui Kurōdo|1=Rokui Kurōdo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392278}}, collectively referred to as {{ill|Tenjōbito|ja|殿上人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjōbito|1=court nobles|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10369708}} alongside those of the Fifth Rank or higher.
In terms of [[:en:Shinkai (divine rank)|divine Ranks]], Shōrokui is the lowest Rank. The [[:en:Samurai|Samurai]] originated from the {{ill|Samuraihon|ja|侍品|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuraihon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} class, which consisted of individuals limited to the Sixth Rank who served as bureaucrats and aides to nobles. The Sixth Rank class was also referred to as {{ill|Aosamurai|ja|青侍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aosamurai|qq=}} (Blue Samurai) due to their {{ill|Po (clothing)|lt=Po robe|ja|袍|ja|袍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Po (clothing)|1=hō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134436881}} being light blue in color.
In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]] and later, individuals holding Ranks such as [[:en:Major (rank)|Major]] were appointed to this position.
Today, Shōrokui is posthumously conferred to individuals such as {{ill|Keishisei|ja|警視正|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keishisei|1=Assistant Commissioner|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632424}} (Senior Police Superintendent) in the {{ill|Japanese police|ja|日本の警察官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese police|1=Japanese police officer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11506095}}, {{ill|Shōbōkan|ja|消防監|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōbōkan|qq=}} (Fire Superintendent) in the {{ill|Firefighting personnel|ja|消防吏員|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Firefighting personnel|1=Uniformed firefighter in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1255655}}, chairs of {{ill|Municipal councils in Japan|ja|日本の地方議会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Municipal councils in Japan|1=local assembly in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505876}} (city, town, and village councils), founders of special institutions and schools, and artists who have made significant contributions in their respective fields.

It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位|1=Junior Fifth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} and above {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|qq=}}.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-Ranks/court-Ranks/</ref> In terms of decorations, it corresponds to the Fifth Class of {{ill|Orders of Merit|ja|勲等|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Orders of Merit|1=Japanese honors system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17990718}} and the Fifth Class of the [[:en:Order of the Golden Kite|Order of the Golden Kite]].
Under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], the Sixth Rank was granted to {{ill|Kaike|ja|介|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaike|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国司|1=kokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1351234}}, who served as the deputy governor of lower provinces. It was classified as a Rank for {{ill|Jige|ja|地下人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jige|1=jige officials|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424047}} (non-court nobles) and was distinctly separate from the Fifth Rank or higher, which denoted nobility ({{ill|Tsūki|ja|通貴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsūki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). Those holding the Sixth Rank were not permitted {{ill|Shōden|ja|昇殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōden|1=Shōden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11511916}} (entry to the palace). However, [[:en:Kurōdo-dokoro|Kurōdo]] (Chamberlains) were exceptions; their roles allowed them palace access, and those of the Sixth Rank were called {{ill|Rokui Kurōdo|ja|六位蔵人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokui Kurōdo|1=Rokui Kurōdo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392278}}, collectively referred to as {{ill|Tenjōbito|ja|殿上人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjōbito|1=court nobles|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10369708}} alongside those of the Fifth Rank or higher.
In terms of [[:en:Shinkai (divine rank)|divine Ranks]], Shōrokui is the lowest Rank. The [[:en:Samurai|Samurai]] originated from the {{ill|Samuraihon|ja|侍品|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuraihon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} class, which consisted of individuals limited to the Sixth Rank who served as bureaucrats and aides to nobles. The Sixth Rank class was also referred to as {{ill|Aosamurai|ja|青侍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aosamurai|qq=}} (Blue Samurai) due to their {{ill|Po (clothing)|lt=Po robe|ja|袍|ja|袍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Po (clothing)|1=hō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134436881}} being light blue in color.
In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]] and later, individuals holding Ranks such as [[:en:Major (rank)|Major]] were appointed to this position.
Today, Shōrokui is posthumously conferred to individuals such as {{ill|Keishisei|ja|警視正|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keishisei|1=Assistant Commissioner|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632424}} (Senior Police Superintendent) in the {{ill|Japanese police|ja|日本の警察官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese police|1=Japanese police officer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11506095}}, {{ill|Shōbōkan|ja|消防監|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōbōkan|qq=}} (Fire Superintendent) in the {{ill|Firefighting personnel|ja|消防吏員|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Firefighting personnel|1=Uniformed firefighter in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1255655}}, chairs of {{ill|Municipal councils in Japan|ja|日本の地方議会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Municipal councils in Japan|1=local assembly in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505876}} (city, town, and village councils), founders of special institutions and schools, and artists who have made significant contributions in their respective fields.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:正六位]][[ko:정6위]][[zh:正六位]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:正六位]][[ko:정6위]][[zh:正六位]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:正六位]][[ko:정6위]][[zh:正六位]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11545372]] Wikidata name: Senior Sixth Rank [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Citation needed|date=August 2015}}
'''Shōrokui''' (正六位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks in Japan|ja|位階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ranks of Japan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and [[:en:Shinkai (divine rank)|divine Ranks]]. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位|1=Junior Fifth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} and above {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|qq=}}.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-Ranks/court-Ranks/</ref> In terms of decorations, it corresponds to the Fifth Class of {{ill|Orders of Merit|ja|勲等|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Orders of Merit|1=Japanese honors system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17990718}} and the Fifth Class of the [[:en:Order of the Golden Kite|Order of the Golden Kite]].
Under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], the Sixth Rank was granted to {{ill|Kaike|ja|介|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaike|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国司|1=kokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1351234}}, who served as the deputy governor of lower provinces. It was classified as a Rank for {{ill|Jige|ja|地下人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jige|1=jige officials|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424047}} (non-court nobles) and was distinctly separate from the Fifth Rank or higher, which denoted nobility ({{ill|Tsūki|ja|通貴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsūki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). Those holding the Sixth Rank were not permitted {{ill|Shōden|ja|昇殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōden|1=Shōden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11511916}} (entry to the palace). However, [[:en:Kurōdo-dokoro|Kurōdo]] (Chamberlains) were exceptions; their roles allowed them palace access, and those of the Sixth Rank were called {{ill|Rokui Kurōdo|ja|六位蔵人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokui Kurōdo|1=Rokui Kurōdo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392278}}, collectively referred to as {{ill|Tenjōbito|ja|殿上人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjōbito|1=court nobles|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10369708}} alongside those of the Fifth Rank or higher.
In terms of [[:en:Shinkai (divine rank)|divine Ranks]], Shōrokui is the lowest Rank. The [[:en:Samurai|Samurai]] originated from the {{ill|Samuraihon|ja|侍品|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuraihon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} class, which consisted of individuals limited to the Sixth Rank who served as bureaucrats and aides to nobles. The Sixth Rank class was also referred to as {{ill|Aosamurai|ja|青侍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aosamurai|qq=}} (Blue Samurai) due to their {{ill|Po (clothing)|lt=Po robe|ja|袍|ja|袍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Po (clothing)|1=hō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134436881}} being light blue in color.
In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]] and later, individuals holding Ranks such as [[:en:Major (rank)|Major]] were appointed to this position.
Today, Shōrokui is posthumously conferred to individuals such as {{ill|Keishisei|ja|警視正|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keishisei|1=Assistant Commissioner|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632424}} (Senior Police Superintendent) in the {{ill|Japanese police|ja|日本の警察官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese police|1=Japanese police officer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11506095}}, {{ill|Shōbōkan|ja|消防監|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōbōkan|qq=}} (Fire Superintendent) in the {{ill|Firefighting personnel|ja|消防吏員|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Firefighting personnel|1=Uniformed firefighter in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1255655}}, chairs of {{ill|Municipal councils in Japan|ja|日本の地方議会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Municipal councils in Japan|1=local assembly in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505876}} (city, town, and village councils), founders of special institutions and schools, and artists who have made significant contributions in their respective fields.
== External links == * [https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/document?lawid=215IO0000000325 位階令] - {{ill|e-Gov Law Search|ja|e-Gov法令検索|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/e-Gov Law Search|qq=}} * [{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 官位相当表] - National Diet Library Digital Archive

'''Shōrokui''' (正六位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks in Japan|ja|位階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ranks of Japan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and [[:en:Shinkai (divine rank)|divine Ranks]]. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位|1=Junior Fifth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} and above {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|qq=}}.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-Ranks/court-Ranks/</ref> In terms of decorations, it corresponds to the Fifth Class of {{ill|Orders of Merit|ja|勲等|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Orders of Merit|1=Japanese honors system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17990718}} and the Fifth Class of the [[:en:Order of the Golden Kite|Order of the Golden Kite]].
Under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], the Sixth Rank was granted to {{ill|Kaike|ja|介|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaike|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国司|1=kokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1351234}}, who served as the deputy governor of lower provinces. It was classified as a Rank for {{ill|Jige|ja|地下人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jige|1=jige officials|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424047}} (non-court nobles) and was distinctly separate from the Fifth Rank or higher, which denoted nobility ({{ill|Tsūki|ja|通貴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsūki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). Those holding the Sixth Rank were not permitted {{ill|Shōden|ja|昇殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōden|1=Shōden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11511916}} (entry to the palace). However, [[:en:Kurōdo-dokoro|Kurōdo]] (Chamberlains) were exceptions; their roles allowed them palace access, and those of the Sixth Rank were called {{ill|Rokui Kurōdo|ja|六位蔵人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokui Kurōdo|1=Rokui Kurōdo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392278}}, collectively referred to as {{ill|Tenjōbito|ja|殿上人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjōbito|1=court nobles|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10369708}} alongside those of the Fifth Rank or higher.
In terms of [[:en:Shinkai (divine rank)|divine Ranks]], Shōrokui is the lowest Rank. The [[:en:Samurai|Samurai]] originated from the {{ill|Samuraihon|ja|侍品|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuraihon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} class, which consisted of individuals limited to the Sixth Rank who served as bureaucrats and aides to nobles. The Sixth Rank class was also referred to as {{ill|Aosamurai|ja|青侍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aosamurai|qq=}} (Blue Samurai) due to their {{ill|Po (clothing)|lt=Po robe|ja|袍|ja|袍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Po (clothing)|1=hō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134436881}} being light blue in color.
In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]] and later, individuals holding Ranks such as [[:en:Major (rank)|Major]] were appointed to this position.
Today, Shōrokui is posthumously conferred to individuals such as {{ill|Keishisei|ja|警視正|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keishisei|1=Assistant Commissioner|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632424}} (Senior Police Superintendent) in the {{ill|Japanese police|ja|日本の警察官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese police|1=Japanese police officer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11506095}}, {{ill|Shōbōkan|ja|消防監|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōbōkan|qq=}} (Fire Superintendent) in the {{ill|Firefighting personnel|ja|消防吏員|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Firefighting personnel|1=Uniformed firefighter in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1255655}}, chairs of {{ill|Municipal councils in Japan|ja|日本の地方議会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Municipal councils in Japan|1=local assembly in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505876}} (city, town, and village councils), founders of special institutions and schools, and artists who have made significant contributions in their respective fields.
== External links == * [https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/document?lawid=215IO0000000325 位階令] - {{ill|e-Gov Law Search|ja|e-Gov法令検索|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/e-Gov Law Search|qq=}} * [{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 官位相当表] - National Diet Library Digital Archive

However, {{ill|Kurōdo-dokoro|en|Kurōdo-dokoro|WD=Q6447696|lt=Kurōdo}} (Chamberlains) were exceptions; their roles allowed them palace access, and those of the Sixth Rank were called {{ill|Rokui Kurōdo|ja|六位蔵人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokui Kurōdo|1=Rokui Kurōdo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392278}}, collectively referred to as {{ill|Tenjōbito|ja|殿上人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjōbito|1=court nobles|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10369708}} alongside those of the Fifth Rank or higher.
In terms of {{ill|Shinkai (divine rank)|en|Shinkai (divine rank)|WD=Q11591025|lt=divine Ranks}}, Shōrokui is the lowest Rank. The {{ill|Samurai|en|Samurai|WD=Q38142|lt=Samurai}} originated from the {{ill|Samuraihon|ja|侍品|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuraihon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} class, which consisted of individuals limited to the Sixth Rank who served as bureaucrats and aides to nobles. In the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period}} and later, individuals holding Ranks such as {{ill|Major (rank)|en|Major (rank)|WD=Q983927|lt=Major}} were appointed to this position.

The Sixth Rank class was also referred to as {{ill|Aosamurai|ja|青侍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aosamurai|qq=}} (Blue Samurai) due to their {{ill|Po (clothing)|lt=Po robe|ja|袍|ja|袍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Po (clothing)|1=hō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134436881}} being light blue in color.

Today, Shōrokui is posthumously conferred to individuals such as {{ill|Keishisei|ja|警視正|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keishisei|1=Assistant Commissioner|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632424}} (Senior Police Superintendent) in the {{ill|Japanese police|ja|日本の警察官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese police|1=Japanese police officer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11506095}}, {{ill|Shōbōkan|ja|消防監|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōbōkan|qq=}} (Fire Superintendent) in the {{ill|Firefighting personnel|ja|消防吏員|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Firefighting personnel|1=Uniformed firefighter in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1255655}}, chairs of {{ill|Municipal councils in Japan|ja|日本の地方議会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Municipal councils in Japan|1=local assembly in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505876}} (city, town, and village councils), founders of special institutions and schools, and artists who have made significant contributions in their respective fields.
