<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:正六位]][[ko:정6위]][[zh:正六位]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:正六位]][[ko:정6위]][[zh:正六位]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:正六位]][[ko:정6위]][[zh:正六位]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11545372]] Wikidata name: Senior Sixth Rank [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Citation needed|date=August 2015}}
'''Shōrokui''' (正六位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks in Japan|ja|位階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ranks of Japan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and {{ill|Shinkai (divine rank)|en|Shinkai (divine rank)|WD=Q11591025|lt=divine Ranks}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位|1=Junior Fifth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} and above {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|qq=}}.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-Ranks/court-Ranks/</ref> In terms of decorations, it corresponds to the Fifth Class of {{ill|Orders of Merit|ja|勲等|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Orders of Merit|1=Japanese honors system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17990718}} and the Fifth Class of the {{ill|Order of the Golden Kite|en|Order of the Golden Kite|WD=Q697797|lt=Order of the Golden Kite}}.
Under the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system}}, the Sixth Rank was granted to {{ill|Kaike|ja|介|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaike|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国司|1=kokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1351234}}, who served as the deputy governor of lower provinces. It was classified as a Rank for {{ill|Jige|ja|地下人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jige|1=jige officials|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424047}} (non-court nobles) and was distinctly separate from the Fifth Rank or higher, which denoted nobility ({{ill|Tsūki|ja|通貴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsūki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). Those holding the Sixth Rank were not permitted {{ill|Shōden|ja|昇殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōden|1=Shōden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11511916}} (entry to the palace). However, {{ill|Kurōdo-dokoro|en|Kurōdo-dokoro|WD=Q6447696|lt=Kurōdo}} (Chamberlains) were exceptions; their roles allowed them palace access, and those of the Sixth Rank were called {{ill|Rokui Kurōdo|ja|六位蔵人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokui Kurōdo|1=Rokui Kurōdo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392278}}, collectively referred to as {{ill|Tenjōbito|ja|殿上人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjōbito|1=court nobles|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10369708}} alongside those of the Fifth Rank or higher.
In terms of {{ill|Shinkai (divine rank)|en|Shinkai (divine rank)|WD=Q11591025|lt=divine Ranks}}, Shōrokui is the lowest Rank. The {{ill|Samurai|en|Samurai|WD=Q38142|lt=Samurai}} originated from the {{ill|Samuraihon|ja|侍品|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuraihon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} class, which consisted of individuals limited to the Sixth Rank who served as bureaucrats and aides to nobles. The Sixth Rank class was also referred to as {{ill|Aosamurai|ja|青侍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aosamurai|qq=}} (Blue Samurai) due to their {{ill|Po (clothing)|lt=Po robe|ja|袍|ja|袍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Po (clothing)|1=hō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134436881}} being light blue in color.
In the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period}} and later, individuals holding Ranks such as {{ill|Major (rank)|en|Major (rank)|WD=Q983927|lt=Major}} were appointed to this position.
Today, Shōrokui is posthumously conferred to individuals such as {{ill|Keishisei|ja|警視正|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keishisei|1=Assistant Commissioner|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632424}} (Senior Police Superintendent) in the {{ill|Japanese police|ja|日本の警察官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese police|1=Japanese police officer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11506095}}, {{ill|Shōbōkan|ja|消防監|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōbōkan|qq=}} (Fire Superintendent) in the {{ill|Firefighting personnel|ja|消防吏員|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Firefighting personnel|1=Uniformed firefighter in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1255655}}, chairs of {{ill|Municipal councils in Japan|ja|日本の地方議会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Municipal councils in Japan|1=local assembly in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505876}} (city, town, and village councils), founders of special institutions and schools, and artists who have made significant contributions in their respective fields.

The {{ill|Samurai|en|Samurai|WD=Q38142|lt=Samurai}} originated from the {{ill|Samuraihon|ja|侍品|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuraihon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} class, which consisted of individuals limited to the Sixth Rank who served as bureaucrats and aides to nobles. revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:正六位]][[ko:정6위]][[zh:正六位]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:正六位]][[ko:정6위]][[zh:正六位]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:正六位]][[ko:정6위]][[zh:正六位]]
{{Citation needed|date=August 2015}}
'''Shōrokui''' (正六位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks in Japan|ja|位階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ranks of Japan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and {{ill|Shinkai (divine rank)|en|Shinkai (divine rank)|WD=Q11591025|lt=divine Ranks}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位|1=Junior Fifth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} and above {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|qq=}}.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-Ranks/court-Ranks/</ref> In terms of decorations, it corresponds to the Fifth Class of {{ill|Orders of Merit|ja|勲等|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Orders of Merit|1=Japanese honors system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17990718}} and the Fifth Class of the {{ill|Order of the Golden Kite|en|Order of the Golden Kite|WD=Q697797|lt=Order of the Golden Kite}}.
Under the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system}}, the Sixth Rank was granted to {{ill|Kaike|ja|介|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaike|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国司|1=kokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1351234}}, who served as the deputy governor of lower provinces. It was classified as a Rank for {{ill|Jige|ja|地下人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jige|1=jige officials|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424047}} (non-court nobles) and was distinctly separate from the Fifth Rank or higher, which denoted nobility ({{ill|Tsūki|ja|通貴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsūki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). Those holding the Sixth Rank were not permitted {{ill|Shōden|ja|昇殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōden|1=Shōden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11511916}} (entry to the palace). However, {{ill|Kurōdo-dokoro|en|Kurōdo-dokoro|WD=Q6447696|lt=Kurōdo}} (Chamberlains) were exceptions; their roles allowed them palace access, and those of the Sixth Rank were called {{ill|Rokui Kurōdo|ja|六位蔵人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokui Kurōdo|1=Rokui Kurōdo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392278}}, collectively referred to as {{ill|Tenjōbito|ja|殿上人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjōbito|1=court nobles|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10369708}} alongside those of the Fifth Rank or higher.
In terms of {{ill|Shinkai (divine rank)|en|Shinkai (divine rank)|WD=Q11591025|lt=divine Ranks}}, Shōrokui is the lowest Rank. The {{ill|Samurai|en|Samurai|WD=Q38142|lt=Samurai}} originated from the {{ill|Samuraihon|ja|侍品|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuraihon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} class, which consisted of individuals limited to the Sixth Rank who served as bureaucrats and aides to nobles. The Sixth Rank class was also referred to as {{ill|Aosamurai|ja|青侍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aosamurai|qq=}} (Blue Samurai) due to their {{ill|Po (clothing)|lt=Po robe|ja|袍|ja|袍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Po (clothing)|1=hō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134436881}} being light blue in color.
In the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period}} and later, individuals holding Ranks such as {{ill|Major (rank)|en|Major (rank)|WD=Q983927|lt=Major}} were appointed to this position.
Today, Shōrokui is posthumously conferred to individuals such as {{ill|Keishisei|ja|警視正|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keishisei|1=Assistant Commissioner|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632424}} (Senior Police Superintendent) in the {{ill|Japanese police|ja|日本の警察官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese police|1=Japanese police officer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11506095}}, {{ill|Shōbōkan|ja|消防監|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōbōkan|qq=}} (Fire Superintendent) in the {{ill|Firefighting personnel|ja|消防吏員|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Firefighting personnel|1=Uniformed firefighter in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1255655}}, chairs of {{ill|Municipal councils in Japan|ja|日本の地方議会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Municipal councils in Japan|1=local assembly in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505876}} (city, town, and village councils), founders of special institutions and schools, and artists who have made significant contributions in their respective fields.
== External links == * [https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/document?lawid=215IO0000000325 位階令] - {{ill|e-Gov Law Search|ja|e-Gov法令検索|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/e-Gov Law Search|qq=}} * [{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 官位相当表] - National Diet Library Digital Archive
{{Court Ranks of Japan}} *

Under the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system|1=Ritsuryō}}, the Sixth Rank was granted to {{ill|Kaike|ja|介|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaike|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国司|1=Kokushi (official)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1351234}}, who served as the deputy governor of lower provinces. In terms of {{ill|Shinkai (divine rank)|en|Shinkai (divine rank)|WD=Q11591025|lt=divine Ranks|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}}, Shōrokui is the lowest Rank. The {{ill|Samurai|en|Samurai|WD=Q38142|lt=Samurai|1=Samurai}} originated from the {{ill|Samuraihon|ja|侍品|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuraihon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} class, which consisted of individuals limited to the Sixth Rank who served as bureaucrats and aides to nobles. The Sixth Rank class was also referred to as {{ill|Aosamurai|ja|青侍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aosamurai|qq=}} (Blue Samurai) due to their {{ill|Po (clothing)|lt=Po robe|ja|袍|ja|袍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Po (clothing)|1=hō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134436881}} being light blue in color.
In the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}} and later, individuals holding Ranks such as {{ill|Major (rank)|en|Major (rank)|WD=Q983927|lt=Major|1=Major (rank)}} were appointed to this position.

It was classified as a Rank for {{ill|Jige|ja|地下人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jige|1=jige officials|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424047}} (non-court nobles) and was distinctly separate from the Fifth Rank or higher, which denoted nobility ({{ill|Tsūki|ja|通貴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsūki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). Those holding the Sixth Rank were not permitted {{ill|Shōden|ja|昇殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōden|1=Q11511916|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11511916}} (entry to the palace). However, {{ill|Kurōdo-dokoro|en|Kurōdo-dokoro|WD=Q6447696|lt=Kurōdo|1=Kurōdo-dokoro}} (Chamberlains) were exceptions; their roles allowed them palace access, and those of the Sixth Rank were called {{ill|Rokui Kurōdo|ja|六位蔵人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokui Kurōdo|1=Q11392278|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392278}}, collectively referred to as {{ill|Tenjōbito|ja|殿上人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjōbito|1=court nobles|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10369708}} alongside those of the Fifth Rank or higher.
In terms of {{ill|Shinkai (divine rank)|en|Shinkai (divine rank)|WD=Q11591025|lt=divine Ranks|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}}, Shōrokui is the lowest Rank. The {{ill|Samurai|en|Samurai|WD=Q38142|lt=Samurai|1=Samurai}} originated from the {{ill|Samuraihon|ja|侍品|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuraihon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} class, which consisted of individuals limited to the Sixth Rank who served as bureaucrats and aides to nobles. The Sixth Rank class was also referred to as {{ill|Aosamurai|ja|青侍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aosamurai|qq=}} (Blue Samurai) due to their {{ill|Po (clothing)|lt=Po robe|ja|袍|ja|袍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Po (clothing)|1=hō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134436881}} being light blue in color.
In the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}} and later, individuals holding Ranks such as {{ill|Major (rank)|en|Major (rank)|WD=Q983927|lt=Major|1=Major (rank)}} were appointed to this position.
Today, Shōrokui is posthumously conferred to individuals such as {{ill|Keishisei|ja|警視正|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keishisei|1=Assistant Commissioner|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632424}} (Senior Police Superintendent) in the {{ill|Japanese police|ja|日本の警察官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese police|1=Japanese police officer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11506095}}, {{ill|Shōbōkan|ja|消防監|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōbōkan|qq=}} (Fire Superintendent) in the {{ill|Firefighting personnel|ja|消防吏員|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Firefighting personnel|1=Uniformed firefighter in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1255655}}, chairs of {{ill|Municipal councils in Japan|ja|日本の地方議会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Municipal councils in Japan|1=local assembly in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505876}} (city, town, and village councils), founders of special institutions and schools, and artists who have made significant contributions in their respective fields.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:正六位]][[ko:정6위]][[zh:正六位]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:正六位]][[ko:정6위]][[zh:正六位]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:正六位]][[ko:정6위]][[zh:正六位]]
{{Citation needed|date=August 2015}}
'''Shōrokui''' (正六位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks in Japan|ja|位階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ranks of Japan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and {{ill|Shinkai (divine rank)|en|Shinkai (divine rank)|WD=Q11591025|lt=divine Ranks|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位|1=Junior Fifth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} and above {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|qq=}}.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-Ranks/court-Ranks/</ref> In terms of decorations, it corresponds to the Fifth Class of {{ill|Orders of Merit|ja|勲等|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Orders of Merit|1=Japanese honors system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17990718}} and the Fifth Class of the {{ill|Order of the Golden Kite|en|Order of the Golden Kite|WD=Q697797|lt=Order of the Golden Kite|1=Order of the Golden Kite}}.
Under the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system|1=Ritsuryō}}, the Sixth Rank was granted to {{ill|Kaike|ja|介|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaike|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国司|1=Kokushi (official)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1351234}}, who served as the deputy governor of lower provinces. It was classified as a Rank for {{ill|Jige|ja|地下人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jige|1=jige officials|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424047}} (non-court nobles) and was distinctly separate from the Fifth Rank or higher, which denoted nobility ({{ill|Tsūki|ja|通貴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsūki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). Those holding the Sixth Rank were not permitted {{ill|Shōden|ja|昇殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōden|1=Q11511916|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11511916}} (entry to the palace). However, {{ill|Kurōdo-dokoro|en|Kurōdo-dokoro|WD=Q6447696|lt=Kurōdo|1=Kurōdo-dokoro}} (Chamberlains) were exceptions; their roles allowed them palace access, and those of the Sixth Rank were called {{ill|Rokui Kurōdo|ja|六位蔵人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokui Kurōdo|1=Q11392278|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392278}}, collectively referred to as {{ill|Tenjōbito|ja|殿上人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjōbito|1=court nobles|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10369708}} alongside those of the Fifth Rank or higher.
In terms of {{ill|Shinkai (divine rank)|en|Shinkai (divine rank)|WD=Q11591025|lt=divine Ranks|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}}, Shōrokui is the lowest Rank. The {{ill|Samurai|en|Samurai|WD=Q38142|lt=Samurai|1=Samurai}} originated from the {{ill|Samuraihon|ja|侍品|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuraihon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} class, which consisted of individuals limited to the Sixth Rank who served as bureaucrats and aides to nobles. The Sixth Rank class was also referred to as {{ill|Aosamurai|ja|青侍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aosamurai|qq=}} (Blue Samurai) due to their {{ill|Po (clothing)|lt=Po robe|ja|袍|ja|袍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Po (clothing)|1=hō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134436881}} being light blue in color.
In the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}} and later, individuals holding Ranks such as {{ill|Major (rank)|en|Major (rank)|WD=Q983927|lt=Major|1=Major (rank)}} were appointed to this position.
Today, Shōrokui is posthumously conferred to individuals such as {{ill|Keishisei|ja|警視正|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keishisei|1=Assistant Commissioner|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632424}} (Senior Police Superintendent) in the {{ill|Japanese police|ja|日本の警察官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese police|1=Japanese police officer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11506095}}, {{ill|Shōbōkan|ja|消防監|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōbōkan|qq=}} (Fire Superintendent) in the {{ill|Firefighting personnel|ja|消防吏員|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Firefighting personnel|1=Uniformed firefighter in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1255655}}, chairs of {{ill|Municipal councils in Japan|ja|日本の地方議会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Municipal councils in Japan|1=local assembly in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505876}} (city, town, and village councils), founders of special institutions and schools, and artists who have made significant contributions in their respective fields.
